rulings.Case Title: TAYLOR KOROBOW VS. SUSAN HARDIN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 27, 2007, LINE 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT MICHAEL ALFARO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PRESTON ADDRESSES PLAINTIFF'S THEORY THAT A PRIOR ACT OF NEGLIGENCE CONTINUES AFTER THE LANDOWNER (AND NECESSARILY THE LANDOWNER'S AGENT) CEASES TO BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE PROPERTY. DEFENDANT'S 1996 STATEMENT TO PLAINTIFF CANNOT FORM THE BASIS FOR A CONTINUING DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF WHEN USING THE LADDER IN 2004 GIVEN THAT DEFENDANT CEASED TO EXERCISE CONTROL OVER THE PROPERTY IN 1999. DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE A DUTY TO ASCERTAIN HOW PLAINTIFF (AS WELL AS ALL OTHER OWNERS) MIGHT USE THE LADDER IN THEIR UNITS. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: TAYLOR KOROBOW VS. SUSAN HARDIN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 27, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT CHANDLER PROPERTIES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE LADDER WAS NOT DEFECTIVE. DEFENDANT DID NOT PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH THE LADDER TO USE TO OPEN THE DOOR. THE FACTS (1.) THAT PLAINTIFF WAS THE PROPERTY MANAGER OF THE COMMON AREA, AND (2.) THE LADDER WAS IN THE COMMON AREA FOR USE BY OWNERS DOES NOT ESTABLISH A DUTY ON THE PART OF DEFENDANT TO INSPECT PLAINTIFF'S UNIT TO ASCERTAIN ALL POSSIBLE WAYS IN WHICH PLAINTIFF COULD USE THE LADDER. PLAINTIFF ASSUMED THE RISK WHEN SHE CHOSE TO USE THE LADDER AND DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE A DUTY TO INSURE THAT THE LADDER WAS FIT FOR WHATEVER TASK PLAINTIFF INTENDED TO UNDERTAKE. HEARING REQUIRED ON DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: SHAYNE MASON VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 27, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, HARRIET HOPF, JOHN FEINER, JEREMY LIEBERMAN, MARK LOONEY, MICHAEL GROPPER, CHARLES YOST AND PEARL TOY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TWENTY DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT PRURITIS RESULTED IN 50% OF CASES (PARAGRAPHS 10 AND 16); THAT EACH DEFENDANT REPRESENTED THAT "HIGH OCCURRENCE RISKS" WERE DISCLOSED (PARAGRAPH 26); AND THAT DEFENDANTS WERE AGENTS OF THE REGENTS (PARAGRAPH 6). =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE ORIENT VS. CONSUMERINFO.COM, INC AKA EXPERIAN CONSUMER DIRECT et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 27, 2007, LINE 10. DEFENDANT CONSUMERINFO.COM, INC.'S, A.K.A. EXPERIAN CONSUMER DIRECT, DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PARAGRAPH 8 REFLECTS THAT NO MISREPRESENTATION WAS MADE AS TO THE CONTENT OF THE CONTRACT AT THE TIME PLAINTIFF SIGNED THE AGREEMENT. BASED ON THE ANALYSIS IN PACIFIC STATE BANK V GREENE, 110 CAL APP 4TH 375, THIS IS A CASE OF PROMISSORY FRAUD AND IS BARRED BY THE PAROL EVIDENCE RULE. =(302/PJM/PB)
